title: Fix Start Anonymously intermittent crash by hardening landing↔experience history sync and improving ErrorBoundary diagnostics

requirements.summary: Prevent intermittent ErrorBoundary crash when re-opening the Experience view via “Start Anonymously” after navigating through login/chat flows.
acceptanceCriteria: From the landing page, clicking "Start Anonymously" always opens the Experience view without triggering the global ErrorBoundary screen.
After entering the login flow (Internet Identity choice screen and/or local auth screen) and visiting the Anonymous Chat tab, clicking "Start Anonymously" again reliably opens the Experience view.
QA can repeat the navigation path multiple times (including browser back) without intermittent failures.

file_operations.description: Create a dedicated hook that owns the Experience open/close state machine and keeps it consistent with window.history (pushState/replaceState + popstate), including safe guards for repeated open/close cycles and navigation that occurs while Experience is open.

Replace ad-hoc showExperience state/history handling with the new useExperienceNavigationState hook so that "Start Anonymously" always results in a valid Experience open state even after visiting login/chat and returning to landing.

Adjust Experience close/back behavior to call the centralized close/back handler from the landing-level navigation state (via prop callback) so leaving the Experience view does not leave behind inconsistent history entries that can later cause a crash.

requirements.summary: Make landing/experience navigation robust by synchronizing MindVaultLandingPage UI state with browser history.state across open/close, back navigation, and repeated cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Closing the Experience view returns the user to the landing view without leaving history/state in an inconsistent condition.
Using the browser back button after opening the Experience view returns to the landing view without triggering an error screen.
Repeated cycles of open Experience -> navigate within Experience (login/chat) -> close/back -> open Experience do not accumulate invalid history states.

file_operations.description: Implement durable history-state semantics (namespaced state key + initialization via replaceState) and popstate handling that sets UI state from event.state (rather than stale closures), ensuring open/close and back always converge to a consistent landing vs experience view state.

Wire open/close actions to the hook so that closing the Experience view either navigates back to the pre-open history entry when appropriate or normalizes state via replaceState, preventing accumulation of invalid/duplicated history states.

Harden transition handling for rapid or repeated showExperience toggles (e.g., ensure timers are cleared and view switching is consistent) so UI transitions cannot desync the visible layer from the underlying navigation state.

requirements.summary: Improve ErrorBoundary diagnostics and make “Try Again” recovery deterministic without changing user-facing English meaning.
acceptanceCriteria: When the ErrorBoundary renders, the console includes the original error plus enough context to debug navigation (at minimum: current URL, history.state, whether Experience was intended to be open).
Clicking "Try Again" results in a deterministic recovery behavior (either successfully re-rendering the app state or performing a full reload), instead of returning to a broken state.

file_operations.description: Add small utilities to safely serialize/print navigation context for debugging (current URL, history.state, and a landing-controlled flag for whether Experience was intended to be open) without throwing during logging.

Enhance componentDidCatch logging to include original error + navigation context (URL, history.state, and intended Experience-open flag) using errorBoundaryDiagnostics, and update “Try Again” to a deterministic recovery (prefer full reload or a clearly defined reset+reload fallback) while keeping the on-screen copy in English and unchanged in meaning.

Set/update a lightweight, global-friendly 'Experience intended open' signal (via errorBoundaryDiagnostics helper) whenever the landing/experience state changes, so ErrorBoundary logs can reliably indicate whether Experience was supposed to be open at crash time.
